creator: michael.osipov@siemens.com
text:
This popped up while working on Bug 244776 with Poudriere.

Consider you have a port using Java and sets
> JAVA_VERSION=8+

now you want to test the port with Poudriere using sets by providing a <setname>-make.conf with the desired testable Java version: 8, 11, 12, 13 and so on.

Now you do:
> poudriere testport -j 121-release-amd64 -p dev -o sysutils/javaservicewrapper -z java13
> poudriere testport -j 121-release-amd64 -p dev -o sysutils/javaservicewrapper -z java8
> poudriere testport -j 121-release-amd64 -p dev -o sysutils/javaservicewrapper -z java11

Actual behavior:
Unfortunately, since the Makefile already defines JAVA_VERSION the value in make.conf is ignored. One cannot test through Poudriere. The only workaround is to remove JAVA_VERSION from the Makefile.

Expected behavior:
If both JAVA_VERSION variables are set, compare the value from make.conf whether it can satify the one from the Makefile.
Example:
- make.conf:JAVA_VERSION=11, Makefile:JAVA_VERSION=8+ => Java 11 is picked
- make.conf:JAVA_VERSION=11, Makefile:JAVA_VERSION=8 => abort, dependency cannot be satisfied
- make.conf:JAVA_VERSION=7, Makefile:JAVA_VERSION=8+ => abort, dependency cannot be satisfied
- make.conf:JAVA_VERSION=13, Makefile:JAVA_VERSION=8 11 => abort, dependency cannot be satisfied
- make.conf:JAVA_VERSION=17, Makefile:JAVA_VERSION=8+ => abort, if Java 17 is not available (dep satisfaction)

I don't expect JAVA_VERSION in make.conf to receive a list. Only a single value w/o the plus sign.

Log files from Poudriere builds can be provided.
